# Figure description: 43.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-VII/43.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **predictive (forecast) geological map** (*Карта прогнозов*) with elements showing the regularities of mineral deposit distribution (*с элементами закономерностей размещения полезных ископаемых*). It is a specialized thematic map combining structural geology, mineralization zoning, and predictive assessment of mineral potential at 1:200,000 scale.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers sheet **М-42-VII** (a 1:200,000 topographic-geological grid cell within the M-42 series covering northern Kazakhstan). It was produced in **1984** by the **Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association** (*Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение*) under the **Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR**. It is identified as **Чертёж № 38** (Drawing No. 38) and corresponds to the broader survey reported in Report 36572 (Smolianova et al., 1983–1984). The stamp indicates it belongs to the **Republican Geological Fund of the Kazakh SSR**, inventory number **36-2б9** (approximately).

The subject is a structural-mineragenic zone in the central portion of the map sheet, likely a folded or anticlinal belt, with delineated predictive mineralization blocks.

---

## Key Content

**Structural Framework:**
- The map displays a prominent **arcuate to sinuous geological structure** — likely a folded anticlinorium or structural zone — running roughly **NW–SE then curving northward**, shown by closely spaced contour-like structural lines (probably depth-to-basement or structural isohypses). Contour values visible include: **800, 900, 1000, 1100, 1200, 1400, 1600, 1800** (likely in metres, representing depth or elevation of a marker horizon).
- The structure has a tightly closed core in the central-western area and opens toward the northeast and southeast.

**Mineralization Zones (red hatching and outlined polygons):**
- Two or three distinct **predictive mineralization blocks** are delineated in **red horizontal hatching**, located along the crest and flanks of the structural high. These are labeled:
  - **1б–2б / 320·10³** — a combined predictive block in the northern portion, with a resource estimate of **320 × 10³** (units not fully visible, likely tonnes of ore or metal).
  - **2б** — a separate southern predictive block, also hatched in red.
  - Roman numerals **I** and **II** are marked within or near these blocks, likely denoting sub-zones or cross-section positions.
- A **diagonal red cross-hatching pattern** extends to the southeast across the map, likely indicating a **fault zone, shear zone, or prospective linear structural element** (possibly a deep fault or suture interpreted from geophysical data).

**Geological Units:**
- Large areas labeled **KZ** (likely **Kaynozoy / Cenozoic** cover) dominate the eastern, southern, and southwestern portions of the map, indicating thick unconsolidated cover masking the basement.
- Exposed or near-surface older rocks are present in the central structural zone, annotated with symbols such as **P₁, P₂** (Permian?), **C, C₁** (Carboniferous), suggesting Paleozoic basement involvement.
- Small isolated outcrop symbols in the northeast corner appear labeled **C, C₁Dm** or similar (Carboniferous–Devonian?).

**Background contours** (thin dashed lines) likely represent **geophysical isolines** (gravity or magnetic field contours) superimposed on the geological base.

## Research Value

This figure synthesizes structural geology and predictive mineralization assessment for sheet M-42-VII, identifying two quantified predictive ore blocks (1б–2б and 2б, with a combined forecast resource of at least 320×10³ units) localized along the axis of a major arcuate structural high interpreted from geophysical and geological data. A geologist or AI research assistant would learn the spatial relationship between deep structural controls (the fold/fault system), Cenozoic cover extent, and the predicted ore-bearing zones, directly answering the question of **where, within this sheet, the highest mineral potential is localized**. The figure is essential for prioritizing exploration targets and connects directly to the seismic and gravity survey results described elsewhere in
